fertilizerNeeds: Monthly feeding with balanced fertilizer during growing season
nativeRegion: Tropical regions of Central Africa to Southeast Asia
culturalUses: Widely used in traditional medicine, culinary dishes, and home gardens across Costa Rica.
culturalUsesES: Ampliamente utilizado en medicina tradicional, platos culinarios y jardines hogareños en toda Costa Rica.
toxicityHumans: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) for culinary use
economicImportance: Highly significant in culinary commercial markets and essential oil extraction.
localSeasonality: Thrives heavily during the rainy season; offers continuous harvest with proper pruning.
warnings: Extremely sensitive to frost and cold drafts
Bolts rapidly if left unpruned in high heat
warningsES: Extremadamente sensible a las heladas y corrientes de aire frío
Florece rápidamente si se deja sin podar en calor intenso
careTips: Water regularly when top 2 centimeters of soil feel dry to the touch.
Requires full sun exposure for at least 6 hours daily.
Pinch off the top pairs of leaves regularly to encourage bushy branching and delay flowering.
careTipsES: Riegue regularmente cuando los 2 centímetros superiores del suelo se sientan secos al tacto.
Requiere exposición a sol completo durante al menos 6 horas diarias.
Pellizque regularmente los pares de hojas superiores para fomentar un crecimiento frondoso y retrasar la floración.

images.caption: Fresh sweet basil growing in a tropical outdoor herb garden
images.altText: Lush green sweet basil plants with broad aromatic leaves growing in a tropical garden setting

changeLog.changes: Added comprehensive schema enhancements including companion planting, pollinator value, harvest info, pests/diseases, soil preferences, multimedia, phenology, climate resilience, taxonomy, and app-specific fields

Implemented all 6 improvement categories: (1) Populated null fields (carbonSequestration, biodiversityScore, invasivePotential, organicCertification), (2) Added CR-specific regionalAdaptations, (3) Expanded facets with difficulty, pollinator, companion, maintenance, (4) Added comprehensive Spanish i18n translations for pests, harvestInfo, culturalUses, careTips, and soilPreferences, (5) Added enrichmentLevel to source object, (6) Added nice-to-have fields (sunlightHoursMin, toxicityPets/toxicityHumans split, daysToMaturity, relatedPlants)

Addressed minor gaps: Added Spanish translations for warnings, companionPlants.benefit/notes, pollinatorValue.specificNotes, and climateSuitability descriptions. Added sunlightHoursMax, enhanced relatedPlants to objects with benefit, populated etymology field, added expectedYieldPerSqMeter to harvestInfo

videoRefs.title: How to Prune Basil for Bushy Growth

etymology: From Greek 'basilikos' meaning 'royal' or 'kingly', referring to the plant's royal status in ancient cultures. Spanish 'albahaca' from Arabic 'al-habaqa'
metaDescription: Sweet Basil (Ocimum basilicum) - A fast-growing aromatic herb perfect for Costa Rican gardens. Learn about care, harvesting, companion planting, and culinary uses.

companionPlants.benefit: Improves flavor and growth, repels hornworms and aphids
companionPlants.benefitES: Mejora el sabor y el crecimiento, repele gusanos cornudos y pulgones
companionPlants.notes: Classic companion planting combination for huertos
companionPlants.notesES: Combinación clásica de plantación compañera para huertos

companionPlants.benefit: Mutually beneficial growth enhancement and pest deterrence
companionPlants.benefitES: Mejora del crecimiento mutuamente beneficiosa y disuasión de plagas
companionPlants.notes: Both thrive in similar warm conditions
companionPlants.notesES: Ambos prosperan en condiciones cálidas similares

companionPlants.benefit: General garden pest repellent, similar water needs
companionPlants.benefitES: Repelente general de plagas de jardín, necesidades de agua similares
companionPlants.notesES: Crea barrera aromática contra las plagas

pollinatorValue.specificNotes: Small white flowers are particularly attractive to native Costa Rican bee species
pollinatorValue.specificNotesES: Las pequeñas flores blancas son particularmente atractivas para las especies nativas de abejas costarricenses

harvestInfo.yieldPerPlant: Approximately 20-30 cups of leaves per season
harvestInfo.yieldPerPlantES: Aproximadamente 20-30 tazas de hojas por temporada
harvestInfo.frequencyES: Semanalmente durante la temporada de crecimiento

tips: Add fresh leaves at the end of cooking to preserve flavor
Pinch flowers to encourage leaf production
Store stems in water like fresh flowers
Flowers are edible with milder flavor than leaves
Use as decorative garnish for dishes

commonPests.name: Aphids
commonPests.symptoms: Sticky residue on leaves, curled or distorted new growth, yellowing leaves
commonPests.symptomsES: Residuo pegajoso en las hojas, crecimiento nuevo curvado o distorsionado, hojas amarillentas
organicControls: Wash off with strong water spray
organicControlsES: Rocíar con solución de aceite de neem
Lavar con rociadora de agua fuerte

commonPests.name: Japanese beetles
commonPests.symptoms: Skeletonized leaves, visible beetles on plant
commonPests.symptomsES: Hojas esqueletizadas, escarabajos visibles en la planta
organicControls: Use row covers during peak beetle season
Plant trap crops like four o'clocks nearby
organicControlsES: Recoger escarabajos a mano temprano en la mañana
Usar cubiertas de hileras durante la temporada pico de escarabajos
Plantar cultivos trampa como las bellas de noche cerca

commonPests.name: Slugs
commonPests.symptoms: Irregular holes in leaves, slime trails on plant and soil
commonPests.symptomsES: Agujeros irregulares en las hojas, rastros de babosa en la planta y el suelo
organicControls: Hand pick at night with flashlight
organicControlsES: Usar barreras de cobre alrededor de las plantas
Aplicar tierra de diatomeas alrededor de la base
Recoger a mano en la noche con linterna

commonPests.name: Downy mildew
commonPests.symptoms: Yellowing leaves with gray-purple fuzz on underside, leaf drop
commonPests.symptomsES: Hojas amarillentas con pelusa gris-púrpura en el envés, caída de hojas
organicControls: Water at base not on leaves
organicControlsES: Mejorar la circulación de aire alrededor de las plantas
Regar en la base, no en las hojas

diseaseResistance: Moderate to downy mildew, susceptible to fusarium wilt

soilPreferences.nutrientNotes: Requires nitrogen-rich soil; supplement with compost or balanced organic fertilizer monthly during growing season
soilPreferences.nutrientNotesES: Requiere suelo rico en nitrógeno; suplementar con compost o fertilizante orgánico equilibrado mensualmente durante la temporada de crecimiento

relatedPlants.benefit: Thyme - similar growing conditions and culinary uses
Mint - aromatic herb for teas and garnishes
Parsley - classic herb combination for cooking

regionalAdaptations.adaptation: Thrives in highland elevations (1000-1500m) with moderate temperatures
regionalAdaptations.notes: Benefits from afternoon shade during peak dry season months

regionalAdaptations.adaptation: Adapts well to high humidity and consistent rainfall
regionalAdaptations.notes: Requires improved drainage to prevent root rot in heavy rains
